objective: Determine whether duplicate depth-two component value digests hide distinct material and local-mobility signatures that could justify a versioned richer agency/value target.
non_negotiables: Do not promote a new clean composition shard from a duplicate-cluster diagnostic.
Do not claim decomposition-benefit model evidence from profile diagnostics alone.
Keep Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 shard hashes stable.
Any next value-rule proposal must be versioned and must state split semantics before it is used for model claims.

tasks.prompt: Add a bounded duplicate-cluster report for the corner-plus-edge profile source. Group budget-safe profiles by depth-two value digest, count duplicate clusters, and expose representative material/local-move signatures without changing shard output.
outputs: Regression test pinning aggregate collapse counts
Stable Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 shard hashes
acceptance: Report exposes budget-safe profile counts, unique digest counts, duplicate cluster counts, and representative signatures.
Report shows whether duplicate value digests hide distinct material/local-move signatures.
No default shard command output changes.

tasks.prompt: Record the duplicate-cluster report and update the claim ledger. If duplicate digests hide distinct material/local-move signatures, constrain the next wave toward a versioned richer target rather than another square-source variant.
outputs: Experiment matrix row with report hash and aggregate counts
acceptance: Docs state that 254 white profiles collapse to 15 digests and 517 black profiles collapse to 14 digests.
Docs state that the largest white and black clusters contain distinct mobility signatures.
Docs require a versioned richer target before treating these signatures as supervision.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_36_duplicate_profile_clusters.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 36 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 36 duplicate-cluster report is valid JSON.
